# Dead Code Survey — Round 18

**Purpose.** Catalogue exports, symbols, flags, routes, and components that
appear unused after Round 17 so that removals can be staged as small,
reversible commits rather than a single mass deletion.

**Re-run.** `./scripts/run_dead_code_survey.sh` rewrites the auto-generated
section at the bottom; the curated triage above the `BEGIN AUTOGENERATED`
marker is preserved across runs.

**Scope.** Two scanners produce the candidate set:

- `ts-prune -p theseus-codex/tsconfig.json` — unused TypeScript exports.
- `vulture noosphere/ --min-confidence 70` — unreachable Python symbols.

**Decision column.**

- `remove` — confirmed dead; queued for an atomic removal commit. Tests
  for the symbol are removed in the same commit as the symbol.
- `keep` — flagged but legitimately retained. Rationale recorded.
- `follow-up` — needs manual confirmation. Default for raw scanner output.

---

## Known false-positive categories

These patterns produce ts-prune / vulture hits but are not dead code. They
are kept by policy and should not be flagged for removal.

### Framework conventions (Next.js)

| Pattern | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| `default` export of `page.tsx`, `route.ts`, `layout.tsx`, `not-found.tsx`, `loading.tsx` | Consumed by the App Router file-system convention; ts-prune cannot see the framework's resolver. |
| `metadata`, `generateMetadata`, `dynamic`, `revalidate`, `runtime` | Next-recognised re-exports. |
| `middleware`, `config` in `src/middleware.ts` | Next-recognised entry points. |
| `default` export of a `*Client.tsx` file referenced via `dynamic(() => import('…'))` | Resolved lazily; ts-prune misses dynamic imports. |
| Top-level `default` of `next.config.ts`, `playwright.config.ts`, `prisma.config.ts` | Tool configs read by the framework's loader. |

### Test-only hooks

Exports prefixed `_reset…ForTests`, `__test`, `__test__`,
`__setMailSendersForTesting`, `manifestPathForTests`, etc. The tsconfig
excludes `src/__tests__/**` and `tests/**` from the compilation graph that
ts-prune walks (see `theseus-codex/tsconfig.json` `exclude`), so test
callers are invisible to the scanner. These hooks are real and load-bearing.

### Schema-contract surfaces

`src/lib/_generated/index.ts` is regenerated from
`packages/shared-schemas/*` by `packages/shared-schemas/scripts/generate_pydantic.py`
and its TypeScript counterpart. The re-exports are part of the cross-package
contract enforced by `.github/workflows/type-contracts.yml`. Do not remove,
even if the local app only happens to import a subset today.

### Design system index

`src/components/design/index.ts` and `src/lib/design/tokens.ts` are barrel
files for the design package. They surface tokens consumed across
`app/`/`components/` via deep imports; not every name needs to be hit on a
ts-prune walk to be load-bearing. Removals must be done at the *definition*
site, not by trimming the barrel.

### Signed-publication artefacts (Round 17 prompt 30)

Artefacts under `public/signed/`, `publications/published/`, and any code
that references signing keys via indirection (e.g. computed paths inside
`scripts/check_signed_artifacts.py`, `noosphere/.../signing/`) are out of
scope for this survey. They look unreferenced because the references go
through filesystem indirection. **Never remove.**

## Feature-flag audit (Round 17)

Boolean env flags discovered by grepping `*_ENABLED` / `FEATURE_*` /
`featureFlag` in `theseus-codex/src/`:

| Flag | Read at | Toggle path | Decision |
|------|---------|-------------|----------|
| `FORECASTS_LIVE_TRADING_ENABLED` | `src/lib/forecastPortfolioData.ts:351` | Gates live-trading portfolio view; toggled in production via env. | keep |
| `THESEUS_X_POSTING_ENABLED` | `src/lib/socialPosting.ts:258` | Gates X (Twitter) draft creation; covered by `src/__tests__/socialPosting.test.ts:42,158`. | keep |
| `THESEUS_SUBSTACK_POSTING_ENABLED` | `src/lib/socialPosting.ts:286` | Gates Substack draft creation; covered by `src/__tests__/socialPosting.test.ts:52`. | keep |

All three flags are reachable from a runtime decision and are exercised
by tests. No orphaned flags found in this round.

---

## Route audit

`find theseus-codex/src/app/api -name route.ts` enumerated 84 route
files. None were imported as a module by client code (Next routes are
addressed by URL, not import), so the live-traffic signal must come from
production logs rather than static analysis. **This requires the next
operator pass:** compare the route list to the last 30 days of access
logs and quarantine any route with zero hits under an `app/api/legacy/`
group with a `Sunset:` response header. That removal is *not* attempted
in this survey because the log data is not present in-repo.

Static-only finding: no route file is obviously orphaned (every route
returns a plausible response and matches a documented surface in
`docs/architecture/API_Envelope_Contract.md` or `docs/operator/`).

---

## Component audit

96 `*.tsx` files live under `src/components/`. The clear *false-positive*
hits in the raw table below are `*Client.tsx` files imported via
`next/dynamic` — those are alive. Components with zero static
references after excluding `*Client.tsx` dynamic loaders should be
verified in a follow-up pass with `grep -r '<ComponentName' src/` per
candidate before deletion.

The deletion that already landed on this branch —
`theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/dashboard/dashboard-keymap.tsx` — is
consistent with this survey's spirit but was driven by a separate
dashboard cleanup (see `coding_prompts/54_dashboard_terminology_and_cleanup.txt`).

---

## Removal log

Removals land as atomic commits; each row here records the commit hash
once landed. **No removals are bundled into the commit that introduces
this survey** — that commit is purely infrastructure (`docs/`, `scripts/`,
`.github/workflows/`), so a bisect across it cannot be confused with a
behavioural change.

## Curated triage (above-the-line)

The rows below are pre-classified. The `Decision` column overrides the
default `follow-up` in the auto-generated table.

| File | Symbol | Decision | Rationale |
|------|--------|----------|-----------|
| `next.config.ts` | `default` | keep | Next loader entry. |
| `playwright.config.ts` | `default` | keep | Playwright loader entry. |
| `prisma.config.ts` | `default` | keep | Prisma loader entry. |
| `src/middleware.ts` | `middleware`, `config` | keep | Next middleware contract. |
| `src/app/**/page.tsx` `default` | — | keep | Next App Router. |
| `src/app/**/layout.tsx` `default`, `metadata` | — | keep | Next App Router. |
| `src/app/**/route.ts` `default` | — | keep | Next App Router (route handlers also export GET/POST/etc — `default` may be a Next 16 quirk). |
| `src/components/*Client.tsx` `default` | — | keep | Dynamic-imported via `next/dynamic`. |
| `src/lib/_generated/index.ts` * | — | keep | Schema-contract re-export. |
| `src/lib/api/envelope.ts` `ENVELOPE_CONTRACT_VERSION` | — | keep | Versioned API envelope; referenced by clients outside this tree. |
| `src/lib/api/parseEnvelope.ts` `parseEnvelope`, `readEnvelope` | — | keep | Cross-package contract helpers. |
| `src/lib/api/round3.ts` `GatedResponse`, `fetchProvenanceForConclusion` | — | keep | Round-3 invariants (see `scripts/check_round3_invariants.py`). |
| `src/components/design/index.ts` * | — | keep | Design barrel; trimming the barrel hides re-exports without removing definitions. |
| `src/lib/design/tokens.ts` * | — | keep | Design token surface. |
| `src/lib/csrf.ts` `CSRF_COOKIE_NAME`, `CSRF_HEADER_NAME`, `issueCsrfToken` | — | keep | Security primitive; referenced indirectly via string keys in middleware and tests. |
| `src/lib/mail.ts` `__setMailSendersForTesting`, `__resetMailSendersForTesting` | — | keep | Test hook (tests excluded from tsconfig). |
| `src/lib/apiKeyAuth.ts` `_resetApiKeyRateLimitsForTests` | — | keep | Test hook. |
| `src/lib/rateLimit.ts` `_resetAllLoginRateLimitsForTests` | — | keep | Test hook. |
| `src/lib/publicAsk.ts` `_resetPublicAskRateLimitsForTests` | — | keep | Test hook. |
| `src/lib/subscribeIpRateLimit.ts` `resetSubscribeIpBucketsForTests` | — | keep | Test hook. |
| `src/lib/calibrationData.ts` `manifestPathForTests` | — | keep | Test hook. |
| `src/lib/hotkeys.ts` `__test` | — | keep | Test hook. |
| `src/lib/methodChangelog.ts` `__test__` | — | keep | Test hook. |
| `src/lib/forecastsTypes.ts` `DECISION_ACTIONS` | follow-up | Defined immediately above `DECISION_ACTION_BLURBS`; appears unreferenced but the array form is the canonical iteration source. Verify before remove. |
| `src/app/(home)/DualPulseSection.tsx` `DUAL_PULSE_BREAKPOINT_MATRIX` | follow-up | Only referenced inside its own file (the export is unused). Verify whether the breakpoint matrix is consumed by docs/snapshots before removal. |
| `noosphere/noosphere/coherence/engine.py` `AutoModel`, `AutoTokenizer` imports (L95) | follow-up | Likely conditional-import pattern (transformers); verify the file's `if TYPE_CHECKING:` and runtime-import branches before removing. |
| `noosphere/noosphere/forecasts/scheduler.py` `default_public_calibration_path` import (L30) | follow-up | Imported helper that may have been moved into another module; confirm reachability. |
| `noosphere/tests/**` unused locals | — | keep | Vulture flags pytest fixtures and `pytest.raises(...) as exc_info` style locals as unused; they are part of pytest contract semantics. |

Any candidate not listed above defaults to `follow-up` in the
auto-generated section. The follow-up bar means: a human verifies
reachability (dynamic import? string-keyed registry? CLI entry point?)
before issuing the removal commit.
